title: Review and setlist: Leslie Odom Jr. brings Broadway to the Boston Pops
author: Shannon Murphy
bullets: Leslie Odom Jr. performed with the Boston Pops at Symphony Hall on May 22, 2026, featuring a mix of Broadway classics and American standards.
The concert marked Odom Jr.'s second collaboration with the Boston Pops, celebrating the orchestra's 140th season and the U.S. Declaration of Independence's 250th anniversary.
Highlights included performances of 'Fly Me To The Moon', 'Wait for It' from Hamilton, and 'A Change is Gonna Come' by Sam Cooke.

title: Chelsea man facing slew of assault charges for alleged violence against young children
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Kener Bautista-Zepeda, 19, from Chelsea, charged with multiple assaults on children, including burning and strangulation, per Suffolk prosecutors.
Incidents involved a 2-year-old and a 3-year-old, with evidence from a camera recording showing violent acts in a child's bedroom.
Bautista-Zepeda fled to Virginia after confrontation, later arrested; awaiting a dangerousness hearing on May 28 without bail.

title: Concord man, 73, convicted of raping 20-year-old in Sandwich restaurant bathroom in 2023
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Elie Jammal, 73, was convicted of raping a 20-year-old woman in a Sandwich restaurant bathroom in 2023.
Jammal received a sentence of 13.5 to 15 years in prison plus 5 years of probation.
The court noted his actions were driven by 'a shocking sense of entitlement.'

title: Newton Church, founded in 1893, is becoming condos
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The Church of the Open Word in Newtonville will be converted into six condominiums and a new five-story building with 26 more units.
Developers purchased the historic 1893 church property for $4 million after plans to convert it into an arts hub fell through.
The project reflects a regional trend of converting former churches into residences due to shrinking congregations and housing demand.

title: 5 players the Celtics could target this offseason outside of Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Kawhi Leonard is a potential trade target for the Boston Celtics this offseason, according to NBA insiders.
LeBron James, an unrestricted free agent, could join the Celtics if he leaves Los Angeles seeking another title.
John Collins and Jaxson Hayes are additional candidates Boston might consider to strengthen their forward and center positions.

title: 2 local college baseball programs advance to NCAA World Series
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Bentley University advanced to the NCAA Division II World Series for the first time by defeating Molloy University in the East Super Regional.
Endicott College continued its streak by advancing to their fourth consecutive Division III championship after beating Salve Regina University.
Bentley and Endicott's opponents and game schedules for their respective NCAA World Series will be announced later.

title: Celtics reportedly plan to work out UConn basketball star ahead of NBA Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Celtics to work out UConn star Alex Karaban pre-draft next week, reports say.
Boston holds No. 27 and No. 40 picks for the 2026 NBA Draft.
Karaban, Southborough native, ranks No. 32 on ESPN's top 100 draft prospects list.

title: Review & setlist: Charlie Puth turns MGM Music Hall into a masterclass in musicianship
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Charlie Puth blended jazz and pop at MGM Music Hall, highlighting his skilled musicianship and vocal ability.
Puth performed a mix of new material from 'Whatever's Clever!' and older hits, engaging the Boston audience.
Opening acts Ally Salort and Daniel Seavey showcased diverse talents, setting the stage for the main performance.

title: Review and setlist: Jakob Dylan’s choice to channel Tom Petty pays off at Beverly Wallflowers show
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: The Wallflowers performed their full 1996 album 'Bringing Down the Horse' at The Cabot in Beverly on May 22, 2026.
Jakob Dylan, embodying a style reminiscent of his father, led the band through hits like 'One Headlight' and 'Sixth Avenue Heartache'.
The concert included a tribute to Tom Petty, with the band covering all tracks from Petty's 1982 album 'Long After Dark'.

title: Here’s where people are moving — and leaving — in Mass., according to new U.S. Census data
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston lost 1,338 residents last year, the largest decline in Massachusetts, reducing its population to 672,973.
Everett and Worcester saw the highest population increases, gaining 2,362 and 1,916 residents respectively.
Massachusetts population growth slowed to 0.2% in 2025, driven by reduced immigration and high housing costs.
